titleStmt.title: Capitula Ecclesiastes in Boulogne, BM, MS 2 (2)

publicationStmt.p: Digital editions of summaries in Medieval Bibles

bibl.author: Donatien de Bruyne
bibl.title: Sommaires, divisions et rubriques de la Bible latine

bibl.editor: Monachi abbatiae pontificiae Sancti Hieronymi in Urbe
bibl.title: Biblia Sacra iuxta Latinam vulgatam versionem ad codicum fidem... Libri Salomonis id est Proverbia Ecclesiastes Canticum Canticorum ex interpretatione sancti Hieronymi cum praefationibus et variis capitulorum seriebus

supportDesc.extent:
II + 299 + III folios
height 512 mm width 350 mm

tagsDecl.rendition: the digital reproduction is b/w, impossible to determine color

front.p:
The series of capitula published here is unknown both to de Bruyne and to the Benedictine edition of the Vulgate, and bears no resemblance to any of the other attested series; picking up and continuing the system of sigla adopted in the Benedictine edition, we label it C_dobips. Among the manuscripts recorded in the DOBiPS database, it appears only in Bl1_2 (Boulogne, BM, MS 2 [2]), that is, the second volume of the so-called "Bible of Saint-André-au-Bois" (late 12th cent., Northern France).
Although in the manuscript the number is usually given at the end of each capitulum, here, for the sake of consistency with the other editions of the project, the number has been placed before the text. Occasionally, a capitulum is numbered twice, for example when its text extends over two lines, each concluding with a number. In these cases, both numbers have also been given before the capitulum.
The point in the text of Ecclesiastes where Bl1_2 places the chapter number corresponding to each capitulum can be accessed either by clicking on the two-page icon next to each capitulum number, or by clicking on the "Passi paralleli" thumbnail (in the right-hand panel of the "Testo di lettura" screen).
Note that the chapter numbering in the text itself is not affected by the errors found in the table of capitula; whenever the table has a wrong number, the correct one is provided after the biblical reference.

item.title: Vbi dicit ecclesiastes quod omnia sint uanitas generationem preterire terram uero in ȩternum stare
De ortu et uertigine solis
Quod omnia flumina a mari ducant originem
Quod nichil sit preter quod fuit et quod fuit idipsum futurum sit
Qualiter se ad omnia contulit inuestiganda

bibl.title: Ecl 1:17 et agnovi quod in his

item.title: Qualiter in cogitatu fatigatur uir sapiens

bibl.title: Ecl 2:1 dixi ego [ergo Bl1_2] in corde meo

item.title: Qualiter proponit letandum sed et hoc uanum
Qualiter ualde ditatus in nichilum computat diuitias

list.item: Ecl 2:11 cumque me convertissem [~ convertissem me Bl1_2]IX- X Qualiter circumspectis omnibus totum esse uanum deprehendit

item.title: Disputatio de stulto et sapiente
Similiter mori doctum ut indoctum
Laborare dissimulat pre tedio fatuorum
Despectu carnalium bonum esse dicit manducare et bibere
Omnia dicit tempus habere et suis spatiis uniuersa transire
Prodit distensionem curiositatis
Ex sententia infirmorum corporalibus heret
Iudicium futurum propalat

list.item: Ecl 3:19 idcirco unus interitus (18)XIX- XX Sententia imperitorum quod idem finis sequatur homines et iumenta

item.title: Consideratio malorum huius uitȩ
Contemplatio inertis et inutilis
Homini frustra sudanti succenset
Commendatio duorum ad monimentum caritatis

app.note: The number is written thus, no doubt due to a palaeographical confusion between X and V.

item.title: Triplicem funem idest assiduum uitium difficile rumpi

app.note: The numbering error (XVI instead of XXVI), which affects all the subsequent capitula, may have been facilitated by the change of column (14va to 14vb) occurring before this capitulum.

item.title: Commendatio deo placentis obedientiȩ
Qua reuerentia coram deo quid loquamur docet
Quod deo uouetur cito soluendum omnia non curanda et pauperibus subueniendum
De peccato auaritiȩ
De diuitiis quȩ seruantur nescitur cui

list.item: Ecl 5:12 est et alia infirmitas (29)XXI- XXII Secundum sententiam infirmorum comedendum et bibendum atque gaudendum

bibl.title: Ecl 5:17 hoc itaque mihi visum est (30)

item.title: De homine diuitias habente et ipsis frui non ualente

bibl.title: Ecl 6:1 est et aliud malum quod vidi (31)

app.note: The final part of the number (IIII) is not visible in the digitisation due to the tight binding of the manuscript and should therefore be understood only as a plausible integration.

item.title: Item de infelicitate hominis quo melior sit abortiuus
Hoc habere plus sapientem ab stulto quod illuc iturus sit ubi uita est
Non necesse est homini ut se maiora querat

app.note: The final part of the number (II) is not visible in the digitisation due to the tight binding of the manuscript and should therefore be understood only as a plausible integration.

item.title: Melius esse ire ad domum luctus quam ad domum conuiuii

app.note: The final part of the number (III) is not visible in the digitisation due to the tight binding of the manuscript and should therefore be understood only as a plausible integration.

item.title: Melius esse a sapiente corripi quam stultorum adulatione decipi

bibl.title: Ecl 7:6 melius est a sapiente corripi (36)

item.title: Vt tardi simus ad iram monet

app.note: The final part of the number (X) is not visible in the digitisation due to the tight binding of the manuscript and should therefore be understood only as a plausible integration.

item.title: Quȩ sit comparatio inter pecuniam et sapientiam
Neminem posse corrigere quem deus despexerit

app.note: The final part of the number (II) is not visible in the digitisation due to the tight binding of the manuscript and should therefore be understood only as a plausible integration.

item.title: Videtur infirmis quod iustus parum iniustus multum uiuat
De cura penes iustum habenda

app.note: The final part of the number (III) is not visible in the digitisation due to the tight binding of the manuscript and should therefore be understood only as a plausible integration.

item.title: Volens effici sapiens sibi uidetur stultus magis

app.note: The final part of the number (V) is not visible in the digitisation due to the tight binding of the manuscript and should therefore be understood only as a plausible integration.

item.title: Virum bonum difficile repperiri mulierem uero nec difficile inueniri bonam
Quod fecerit deus hominem sapientem
Sapientiȩ inuigilandum et ab illa non recedendum

app.note: The final part of the number (VIII) is not visible in the digitisation due to the tight binding of the manuscript and should therefore be understood only as a plausible integration.

item.title: Quȩ retributio maneat precepta dei custodientem

app.note: The final part of the number (IX) is not visible in the digitisation due to the tight binding of the manuscript and should therefore be understood only as a plausible integration.

item.title: Non esse in hominis potestate utrum moriatur uel non

bibl.title: Ecl 8:8 non est in hominis dicione prohibere (47)

item.title: De impunitate qua se peccatores tuentur
De prosperitate stultorum et aduersitate iustorum

app.note: The final part of the number (I) is not visible in the digitisation due to the tight binding of the manuscript and should therefore be understood only as a plausible integration.

item.title: Post hec quasi motus leticiam predicat temporalem
Quanta sit difficultas sapientiam inuenire

bibl.title: Ecl 8:16 est homo qui diebus ac noctibus (51)

app.note: The final part of the number (II) is not visible in the digitisation due to the tight binding of the manuscript and should therefore be understood only as a plausible integration.

item.title: Quod ignoratur quid quisque mereatur amorem uel odium
De inequalitate euentus bonorum uel malorum
Repetit arbitrium infirmorum
Commendatio caritatis

app.note: The final part of the number (III) is not visible in the digitisation due to the tight binding of the manuscript and should therefore be understood only as a plausible integration.

item.title: Quod cessare quis non debeat eo quod mors non tardet
Contemplatio sapientissima non esse cuiuspiam quod habere uidetur
Ciuitatem posse saluari per unum uirum sapientem
Qualiter melius uerba sapientis audiuntur
Nil esse comparandum sapientiȩ

app.note: LII instead of LIII in the manuscript.

item.title: Temptanti diabolo non cedendum
De principatu malorum
Malafacientem incidere in malum
Vituperatio detractoris
Multum loqui stultorum est
Beatificat terram regem habentem idoneum
Auaros et munera suscipientes denotat

bibl.title: Ecl 10:19 et pecuniae oboedient [obediunt Bl1_2] omnia (67)

item.title: Pane lacrimarum et compunctione lectionis uti decet
Religioso non esse curandum quid quisque loquatur
In iuuentute bene operandum et in senectute nunquam cessandum
In prosperitate uitȩ longioris cogitandum finis nostri
Predicat sententiam infirmorum quasi ad certum
Non cedendum irȩ nec obediendum adolescentiȩ

bibl.title: Ecl 11:10 aufer iram a corde tuo (73)

item.title: Reminiscendum semper creatoris nostri dei
Comparatio fidelis populi et memoria futuri iudicii
Cogitandum de morte ante mortem
Finis omnium deum timere et sciendum quia deus unumquemque secundum opus suum iudicabit
